title: The one handshake
sub: which single new relationship most shrinks our distance to the whole market
headline: The best single new tie is Cognition (Devin) — that one edge cuts AoC's distance to the whole market by 13.4%. It is nearly a tie: Anthropic sits 0.005 points behind, and the whole top-10 spans 0.28 — so pick the hub you can actually reach.

prose.intro: Agents of Chaos touches this map through five edges, all competitor ties. Suppose we could add exactly one new relationship — a design partner, an investor, a platform integration. Which one would pull us closest to the whole market at once, not just to one company?
prose.how: Treat the graph as an electrical circuit: every edge is a resistor, and the effective resistance between two companies measures how hard it is to travel between them through all routes at once. Many short parallel paths mean low resistance. We score AoC's position as the sum of its resistances to all 170 other companies in the connected core. Then, for each of the 165 companies we have no tie to, we ask: add that one edge — what is the exact new score? (An algebraic shortcut answers this exactly for every candidate at once.) The winners are all hubs, and nearly interchangeable ones: AoC is peripheral enough that even the worst candidate cuts 7.2%, but hubs nearly double that. The comparison column scores the same edge by how much it shrinks resistance between all pairs of companies. That objective prefers rescuing stranded nodes — its winner (Multimodal (AgentFlow), a one-edge pendant, −2.7% global) is the single worst edge for AoC, #165 of 165.
prose.method: Resistance distance per Klein & Randić (1993); global objective is the Kirchhoff index Kf = n·tr(L⁺). L⁺ computed once via dense numpy.linalg.pinv (hermitian) on the 171-node largest connected component of the all-edges company graph (unweighted, unverified edges included). Adding edge (a,u) updates L by +(e_a−e_u)(e_a−e_u)ᵀ; the pseudoinverse follows by Sherman–Morrison, exact because e_a−e_u is orthogonal to the Laplacian null space (cf. Ranjan, Zhang & Boley 2014). Exhaustive over all 165 non-neighbors; the top-3 deltas verified against brute-force pinv recomputation to 1e-8. The 17 companies outside the core are unreachable (infinite resistance) and excluded.

caveat: All of this depends on the current edge inventory: AoC's 5 edges are all competitor ties, so “distance to market” is measured through the rivalry layer. Unverified edges count as wire, and the 17 companies outside the connected core are not scored.
